behaviors.description: Migrations are applied in ascending numeric order determined by the filename prefix.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module handoff/wiring error (discovery -> filename parse/sort -> execution order). Required setup: multiple migration files on disk plus a real runner invocation and a DB-backed applied-order assertion. Unit tests of sort logic alone can miss wiring bugs (e.g., discovery order not passed through). Rejected System: behavior text does not require subprocess/OS edge behavior beyond local filesystem/DB. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp migrations directory with out-of-order filenames (e.g., 001, 010, 002), run the migration runner once against a test Postgres DB, and assert `_migrations` rows (or side-effect tables) show execution order 001 -> 002 -> 010.

behaviors.description: A migration that has already been applied is not re-executed on a subsequent run.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module interaction/state persistence error between migration execution and `_migrations` metadata read on next run. Required setup: run runner twice against same DB and verify second run skips previously recorded migration. Unit logic for `isApplied` can pass while real persistence/wiring fails. Rejected System: no required subprocess/network behavior in the behavior text. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run runner against a DB with one migration that inserts a sentinel row; run runner again; assert sentinel is inserted once and `_migrations` contains one record for that version.

behaviors.description: When a migration statement fails, its changes are rolled back automatically and no further migrations in the same run are attempted.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: transaction/runner control-flow integration error (rollback + stop-on-failure). Required setup: DB transaction semantics plus at least one failing migration followed by another migration to confirm halt. Pure unit tests of error handling can miss real rollback behavior. Considered System because DB behavior is involved, but rejected: this is catchable with in-process runner wiring to local Postgres without subprocess/OS-specific behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Prepare migrations where `001` partially writes then errors, and `002` would create a known table. Execute runner and assert writes from `001` are absent (rolled back), `002` was not applied, and `_migrations` contains neither `001` nor `002` as successful (or only prior successful versions).

behaviors.description: When two instances of the runner start at the same time against the same database, each migration is applied exactly once across both instances.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: environment/external-system concurrency error across separate processes (race conditions, lock timing, transaction isolation interactions). Required setup: two real runner processes started concurrently against the same DB. Mocking/in-process fakes can hide inter-process scheduling and lock contention issues. Rejected Integration due explicit behavior text: "separate processes" and "start at the same time" require real subprocess/OS scheduling. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn two OS processes running the migration command simultaneously against one test Postgres instance; wait for both to finish; assert each migration side effect exists exactly once and `_migrations` has one record per version with no duplicates.

behaviors.description: On the very first run against a fresh database, the `_migrations` table is created before any migration file is applied.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: initialization ordering/wiring error between metadata bootstrap and migration execution. Required setup: fresh DB + runner + migration execution path to verify bootstrap order. Unit tests of `ensureMigrationsTable()` do not prove it is called before applying files. Rejected System: no subprocess/network requirement beyond local DB/filesystem. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fresh Postgres schema with a first migration that can only succeed if metadata bootstrap happened first (or capture SQL statements/order); run runner once; assert `_migrations` exists and first migration was applied afterward.
